BEST ELEVEN Sports Group
Private & Confidential
Investor Presentation · June 2026

Building
South America's
Premier
Multi-Club Group.

A USD 21M equity opportunity in Best Eleven Sports Group — the operating platform redefining how football assets are acquired, developed and monetised across South America.

4
Controlled clubs
4
Countries
20+
Years operating
$21M
Investment raise
Argentina · Paraguay · Uruguay · USA
bestelevensportsgroup.com
01 — Why Football

A $50bn market
driven by structural scarcity.

Football is one of the largest, most liquid and fastest-growing global sports markets. Crisis-resilient. AI-resistant. Structurally non-cyclical.

Capital moves late in football — driven by hype, urgency and fear of missing out. We operate early, before the market prices the asset.

~40%
Transfer value CAGR South American player values have compounded at approximately 40% year over year.
$50B
Global football market Media rights, sponsorships, ticketing and player trading — a market that grew through multiple recessions and a global pandemic.
6+
Value realisation from 6 months Short investment cycles with asymmetric upside. Entry at early-stage pricing, exit at international market pricing.
02 — The Vision

We are building the Red Bull model for South American football — a vertically integrated, multi-country operating platform that turns early access into compounding institutional value.

Red Bull Football Group
  • Multi-club platform across 6 countries
  • Centralised scouting & data infrastructure
  • Internal player development pipeline
  • Coordinated entry from lower to elite divisions
  • Brand leverage across all assets
  • Group-level strategic exits
→
Best Eleven Sports Group — Our Model
  • Operating platform across 4 countries (expanding)
  • Proprietary scouting system: 11,000+ players monitored
  • Internal player rotation across controlled clubs
  • Assets in 3rd through 1st division — full ladder coverage
  • 20+ years of relationship capital in football markets
  • Player sales, club sales, and group-level exit strategies

The Red Bull model was built over two decades. We have already built the foundation — operating clubs, proven exits, institutional relationships. What we are raising capital for is scale acceleration.

The Production Model

The next generation of talent
won't come only from scouting opportunities.
It will come from our clubs' own formation.

Early identification in Argentina, Uruguay and Paraguay gives us access to talent before the market sees it. We develop players through Best Eleven's methods, values and infrastructure - so that tomorrow's transfers are not the result of scouting, but of players who grew up inside our system.

01
Detection
Raw Material
Early identification. Network of scouts and coaches. Focus on regions and age groups the market has not yet priced in.
→
02
Manufacturing
Development
Own training facilities. Best Eleven methodology. Progressive competitive exposure across 4 countries.
03
Export
Promotion and Sale
A player developed inside Best Eleven reaches the market with a track record, competitive context and proven value. Higher price. Higher demand.
$

05 — Rubio Ñu, Paraguay

Copa
Libertadores
by 2027.

We took Rubio Ñu from the 2nd division to the 1st in our first year of management. Squad value grew from under USD 1M to over USD 10M. The club is already self-sustaining via USD 1.8M in annual TV rights. We now position it for its next phase: continental competition and systematic player exports at scale.

Now

Established in Paraguay 1st Division

Squad value > USD 10M. Active player sales pipeline. Network of club relationships across South America and Europe.

2027

Copa Libertadores classification

8 teams qualify to international competitions. Libertadores prize money: ~USD 3.5M. Sudamericana: ~USD 1.5M. Full squad visibility to European scouts.

2028+

USD 10M+ per year in player sales

Permanent top-5 club in Paraguay. Referent developer and exporter of talent. Annual cup prize income of ~USD 5M. Self-sustaining revenue model.

Proven execution

Mariano Ramos — signed at zero cost, loaned with option at USD 320K for 70%. First outgoing transfer to a Libertadores-level club. Squad rebuilt from under USD 1M to over USD 10M in two years.

Strategic Advantages
SAD Structure
100% owned. Fully cleaned up. Operationally independent from original civil association. Clean cap table from day one.
TV Rights Leverage
+600% increase upon promotion to 2nd Division. In Primera División: USD 2M/year — the club becomes fully self-sustaining.
Underdeveloped Territory
Western Montevideo focus. All major clubs concentrate efforts east of the city. First-mover advantage in talent identification and youth recruitment.
Federation Infrastructure Support
Dedicated budget for facility improvements starting 2027 for clubs remaining in the division. External capital contributes to a club already receiving institutional support.
